headline: Teradyne (TER) Valuation Check After Strong Multi‑Month Share Price Rally
summary: Teradyne (TER) has been drawing fresh attention after a strong recent run, with the stock up about 27% over the past month and roughly 68% over the past 3 months. See our latest analysis for Teradyne. The recent surge builds on a longer period of strength, with a 1 year total shareholder return of about 428% and a 5 year total shareholder return near 197%, signaling strong momentum in addition to the recent 86% year to date share price return. headline: Is AI-Fueled Testing Momentum And New JV Altering The Investment Case For Teradyne (TER)?
summary: Teradyne recently reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, highlighting robust AI-driven demand across its Semiconductor Test, Product Test, and Robotics segments, while also forming a joint venture with MultiLane to address AI data center needs and expanding its long-standing automation collaboration with Flex. Together with legal action to protect Universal Robots’ software and increased visibility at industry forums, these moves underscore Teradyne’s efforts to deepen its role in AI...

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Teradyne (TER) After Its 435% One Year Surge?
summary: If you are wondering whether Teradyne at around US$379.93 still makes sense for your portfolio, you are not alone. The recent share price puts the focus firmly on what you are paying versus what you might be getting. The stock has returned 3.9% over the last 7 days, 30.6% over the last 30 days, 83.0% year to date and 434.9% over the last year. This naturally raises questions about how much future upside or risk is already reflected in the price. Recent coverage has centered on Teradyne's...

headline: Flex and Teradyne Robotics Expand Partnership to Scale Intelligent Automation Across Global Manufacturing
summary: Flex (NASDAQ: FLEX) and Teradyne Robotics are expanding their collaboration to accelerate intelligent automation across global manufacturing. Under the expanded relationship, Flex plays a dual role by deploying Teradyne Robotics solutions within its own production facilities while manufacturing key robotics components that enable scalable automation deployments for Teradyne Robotics customers worldwide.
